symptoms.symptom_name: Severe delayed post-exertional crash after minor exertion

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A disproportionate, delayed symptom flare triggered by minor exertion is the defining feature of PEM in ME/CFS and the Long COVID phenotype.

symptoms.symptom_name: Profoundly unrefreshing sleep (14 hours, no recovery)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Unrefreshing sleep is a core diagnostic feature of ME/CFS.

symptoms.symptom_name: Cognitive impairment — brain fog, aphasia, slowed processing

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Cognitive dysfunction ('brain fog') is a core ME/CFS feature and worsens markedly during a crash.
Acute language difficulty can also be a stroke symptom; the global/symmetrical (not focal) pattern is what discriminates PEM from stroke.

symptoms.symptom_name: Global, symmetrical weakness with NO focal/one-sided deficit (carer-observed FAST-negative)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A symmetrical, global picture with no facial droop or single-limb weakness is not the pattern of an acute focal stroke; this is the key discriminator that permits safe home management.
Diffuse whole-body weakness fits a PEM crash rather than a localised neurological lesion.

symptoms.symptom_name: Marked photophobia and phonophobia (sunglasses in a blacked-out room)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sensory hypersensitivity to light and sound is characteristic of a severe ME/CFS crash.

symptoms.symptom_name: Afebrile, no chills; normal skin temperature to touch (pertinent negative for sepsis)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of fever, rigors and any perfusion abnormality makes a septic driver of the collapse unlikely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal neurological examination — intact cranial nerves, symmetrical reflexes, normal (weak) tone, no focal deficit

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The source clinician's in-person neuro exam found no focal deficit — sealed clinician measurement; the AI Doctor infers the non-focal pattern from carer report, not from an examination it can perform.

symptoms.symptom_name: Stable vital signs (BP 105/65, HR 65, RR 14, SpO2 98%, Temp 36.4 C)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal, stable haemodynamics and normothermia — sealed clinician measurement; excludes septic/haemodynamic emergency but is not obtainable in telehealth without a home device.

symptoms.symptom_name: Mildly tender, palpable cervical lymph nodes (post-viral)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Tender lymphadenopathy is a recognised minor feature of ME/CFS; requires palpation, so sealed and not obtainable via chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: Recurrent prior crashes on a known post-viral (Long COVID) background

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: An established diagnosis with a recognised recurrent crash pattern strongly anchors this presentation as another PEM crash rather than a new emergency.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Together — an exertional trigger, unrefreshing sleep, cognitive fog and sensory hypersensitivity on a known post-viral background — these identify a PEM crash with high confidence.
A negative FAST/focal screen plus afebrile stable physiology is what safely excludes the emergency mimics and makes supported home management correct.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Carer-assisted FAST / focal-deficit check (face symmetry, arm drift, speech)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The single most important bedside discriminator obtainable in telehealth — confirms the deficit is global, not focal, before agreeing to home management.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Home vital signs if a device is available (temperature, and pulse oximeter/BP cuff)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: If the household has a thermometer/oximeter, patient-obtainable readings add reassurance against fever/hypoxia; must not be required or fabricated if no device exists.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Routine GP work-up at follow-up if not recently done (FBC, CRP/ESR, TFTs, iron studies, B12, coeliac screen) to exclude alternative fatigue causes
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Standard ME/CFS/Long COVID baseline screen — arranged longitudinally by the GP, not urgently during the crash.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: CT/MRI brain and acute stroke work-up
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not indicated for a global, non-focal PEM crash with a negative FAST screen; would only be warranted if a focal deficit or reduced consciousness emerged (the ESC-001/ESC-002 triggers).
